content.name: AIGP Creator v1.0.0 — Generator
content.summary: Generates AISOP files from research plans. Supports MF1-MF16 multi-file audit (incl. MF15 output contract enforcement, MF14 cross-module field consumer validation), MF4 NAME+VALUE+PATH consistency, and granular traffic light classification, extension-aware STANDARD loading, I11 identity verification, I12 tool annotation consistency, AT1-AT6 threat taxonomy integration.
content.description: Generator module. Creates AISOP JSON from plans, runs three-dimensional quality tests (Cognitive/Intrinsic/Detail), classifies fixes by traffic light (green/yellow/red), and saves drafts. Supports extension-aware STANDARD loading, I11 Program Identity Verification, I12 Tool Annotation Consistency, AT1-AT6 threat taxonomy integration, MF1-MF16 multi-file audit with output contract enforcement, prompt template management, compound Pattern B+E composition guidance, metadata-only regeneration mode, GenerateToolDirs for Pattern G programs, ThreeDimTest automated/subjective split with lint_report, LICENSE FIELD generation, D8-LICENSE semantic check, D4-RANGE cross-reference staleness detection, and STANDARD RANGE TRACE. Token estimate: ~25000 tokens per invocation (AISOP generation + ThreeDimTest).

plan.agentic_prompt: Accept from Research Propose. Null if modifying existing.

modified_file_path.agentic_prompt: Accept from ModifyFlow for re-test. Single file: file path string. Multi-file (Pattern B+): directory path — Generate will scan for all .aisop.json files within.

evolution_plan.agentic_prompt: Accept from EvolveFlow. Contains confirmed LEVEL_A/B/C changes. Null if not evolution.

evolution_context.agentic_prompt: Accept from Pipeline. Contains old_version_context for evolution verification. Null if Create mode or Modify-only. Schema per STANDARD.data_contracts.evolution_context.

module_hashes.agentic_prompt: Accept from Pipeline PipelineStart. Content hashes of target sub-module files for incremental generation. Keys are module_ids, values are SHA-256 hashes. Null if not Pattern B+ or not Evolve mode.

content.system_prompt:
AISOP code generator and quality tester. Produce structurally valid output. Enforce Mermaid-function synchronization rigorously. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.

aisop.main:
graph TD
    Start[Plan or Modified File] --> RouteGate{Route?}
    RouteGate -- New Plan --> ReadTemplate[Read AISOP Template]
    RouteGate -- Modified File --> ThreeDimTest[Three-Dimensional Test]
    ReadTemplate --> Generate[Generate AISOP Draft]
    Generate --> ThreeDimTest
    ThreeDimTest --> TrafficClassify[Traffic Light Classification]
    TrafficClassify --> PostGenCheck{Unresolved Red?}
    PostGenCheck -- Yes --> PauseHuman[Pause for Human]
    PostGenCheck -- No --> ApplyFixes[Apply Green+Yellow Fixes]
    PauseHuman -- Approved --> ApplyFixes
    PauseHuman -- Rejected --> SaveDraft[Save Draft with Warnings]
    ApplyFixes --> SaveDraft
    SaveDraft --> ReflectionCheck[Reflection Check]
    ReflectionCheck --> endNode((End))

Start.step1: Receive plan or modified_file_path. Pass to RouteGate.
Start.constraints: (A) Type guard — string/object only. (B) Size guard — plan >500KB -> reject. (C) Injection guard — sanitize paths. (D) Path guard — reject any path not resolving within workspace_dir (including absolute paths outside workspace and '..' traversal). (E) Encoding guard — NFC normalize.
Start.Error: Invalid input -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: request re-supply -> inform.

RouteGate.step1: Route: plan non-null -> ReadTemplate. modified_file_path non-null -> ThreeDimTest.
RouteGate.Error: Ambiguous input -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: default ReadTemplate route -> inform.

ReadTemplate.step1: TEMPLATE DISCOVERY: Load AIGP_Standard.core.aisop.json from pipeline_context.creator_directory (set by main.aisop.json PipelineStart). DO NOT scan workspace_dir for STANDARD files — Creator's own directory is the authoritative source. If pipeline_context.creator_directory is not set (standalone mode), fallback: locate directory containing this generate.aisop.json file. Read AIGP_Standard.core.aisop.json. Extract quality_checklist and extension_registry. EXTENSION LOADING (per core extension_registry.loading_protocol): (1) ALWAYS load AIGP_Standard.security.aisop.json — security checks mandatory, (2) load AIGP_Standard.ecosystem.aisop.json if target Pattern >= B, (3) load AIGP_Standard.performance.aisop.json if in pipeline context or quality_threshold='strict'. Merge extension quality_checklist items into core checklist. Verify all loaded extensions have matching template_version.
ReadTemplate.step2: Read AIGP_Protocol.md from same directory. Apply Pattern selection: 1 function -> A, 2+ -> B, 2+ with shared logic -> C, sub-modules -> D.
ReadTemplate.step3: Extract complexity + archetype from plan. Finalize generation strategy.
ReadTemplate.step4: PROMPT TEMPLATE MANAGEMENT: read prompt_templates.json from aisop_creator directory (same location as AIGP_Standard.core.aisop.json). If found, load domain-appropriate templates: system_prompt templates, Error field format templates, boundary rule templates, constraints templates. Apply loaded templates during generation for consistency. If prompt_templates.json not found, use built-in defaults — this is expected for most installations. Template format: { templates: { system_prompt: string, error_field: string, boundary_rule: string, constraints: string }, domain_overrides: { [domain]: { ...partial template overrides } } }.
ReadTemplate.constraints: Template not found -> use AI knowledge as fallback. Discovery priority: (1) same directory as generate.aisop.json, (2) scan workspace_dir subdirectories, (3) AI knowledge. (D) Path guard — validate discovered paths within workspace_dir, reject '..'.
ReadTemplate.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: use built-in STANDARD knowledge with 'unverified template' note -> inform.

Generate.step1: TOOL ANNOTATIONS: for each tool declared in the plan, generate annotations object with read_only, destructive, idempotent, open_world flags based on tool behavior. Defaults: all false. Overrides: file_system with write → read_only=false; google_search/web_browser → open_world=true, read_only=true, idempotent=true; tools with delete operations → destructive=true. JSON SCHEMA GENERATION: for each new module, auto-generate json_schema (JSON Schema Draft 2020-12) describing the module's output structure. Derive from: (a) declared parameters with type annotations, (b) output fields in function steps, (c) STANDARD data_contracts if output maps to known contract. Generate AISOP: [system.content] + [user.content.aisop.main Mermaid] + [user.content.functions]. Apply fractal pattern (A-D). NULL HANDLING: if downstream modules will receive research_context=null (e.g., standalone Generate without Pipeline), ensure generated AISOP's Modify-consuming functions include null-safe fallback logic. MULTI-FILE: if plan.multi_file=true, identify affected sub-modules and generate changes per file. COORDINATION ORDER: sub-modules first (bottom-up), then main routing file last. Ensure cross-file data contracts (parameter names, output formats) remain consistent. INCREMENTAL GENERATION: if module_hashes provided, compare each sub-module's current hash against evolution_plan changes. Sub-modules with no planned changes AND unchanged hash -> skip regeneration, preserve existing file. Only regenerate sub-modules that: (a) have planned LEVEL_A/B/C changes targeting them, OR (b) depend on a changed module's output (MF4 cross-file contract affected). CACHE SAFETY: if quality_threshold='strict', disable incremental skip and force full regeneration for maximum correctness assurance. Report skipped modules in generation_report: { skipped: [module_id], regenerated: [module_id], reason: { module_id: string } }. METADATA-ONLY MODE: when evolution_plan.metadata_only_mode=true (all accepted changes target AIGP.md only, no .aisop.json functional changes), activate metadata-only field updates for .aisop.json files: (a) for each .aisop.json file, update ONLY metadata fields — system.content.name (version string), system.content.version, and system.content.description (version attribution) — without regenerating functional content (Mermaid graph, functions, steps, constraints, Error fields), (b) preserve all existing functional content byte-for-byte, (c) record in generation_report: { metadata_only_mode: true, files_metadata_updated: [string], functional_content_preserved: true }. GUARD: if any evolution_plan change targets functional content (new steps, new constraints, new Mermaid nodes, new functions), disable metadata_only_mode and proceed with normal generation. FALLBACK DETECTION: if metadata_only_mode is not explicitly set but evolution_plan contains ONLY changes with affected_modules=['AIGP_MD'], auto-activate metadata_only_mode with advisory log. This avoids unnecessary full regeneration risk when only governance metadata changes. AIGP.md GENERATION: for new creations, generate AIGP.md with: all 12 required frontmatter fields (6 governance + 6 project), governance statement body section, feature overview body section, usage instructions body section, AIGP closing seal. Status set to 'draft' for new creations. LICENSE FIELD: prompt user for SPDX license identifier (per STANDARD.license_spec); default 'proprietary' if not specified. If license='proprietary', require terms_url or contact (at least one). Common choices: MIT (permissive), Apache-2.0 (patent protection), GPL-3.0-only (copyleft), CC-BY-4.0 (documentation), proprietary (commercial). For evolutions, preserve existing license field unless user explicitly requests change.
Generate.step2: If evolution_plan provided, implement confirmed changes: LEVEL_A changes (user-confirmed structural changes — Pattern upgrade, module add/remove, tool changes). LEVEL_B changes (user-confirmed or engine-recommended functional changes). LEVEL_C changes (auto-applied incremental improvements). EVOLUTION VERIFICATION: if evolution_context provided, cross-check each implemented change against evolution_context.old_version to verify the change actually modifies the intended target (e.g., if LEVEL_B says 'add DependencyCheck node', verify the node was actually added and connected correctly). Flag any evolution_plan item that could not be verified as implemented. ROUTING PARAMETER AUTO-CREATION: when LEVEL_A adds a new sub-module, check existing sub-modules for routing parameter patterns. If existing sub-modules receive explicit routing parameters from main (e.g., record receives operation_type, report receives report_type), create an equivalent explicit routing parameter for the new sub-module (e.g., analysis_type for a new analysis module). Add the parameter to: (1) the new sub-module's parameters section, (2) the main router's delegation node step that passes parameters, (3) the new sub-module's ModeGate/routing function. If no existing routing parameter pattern exists (all sub-modules use user_request inference), skip this step. TIER-APPROPRIATE FORM: Simple/Standard AISOP -> prefer adding new steps within existing functions. Professional AISOP -> may add new nodes/routes/functions. Evolution items must NOT be silently dropped. FIX REGRESSION PROTECTION: when rewriting an existing file during evolution, before overwriting any constraints, Error, or description text, compare new text against old file's text. If the old text contains a more precise or qualified version of a phrase that the new text generalizes (e.g., old: 'Read-only data loading (budget writes handled by WriteBudget)' vs new: 'Read-only'), PRESERVE the old precise wording. Detection: for each constraints/Error field being rewritten, diff old vs new — if old text contains additional qualifiers, scoping phrases, or cross-references that new text omits, flag as potential regression and preserve old wording. This prevents evolution from undoing fixes applied in prior versions.
Generate.step3: MERMAID SYNC: Mermaid nodes <-> functions 1:1. All endNode reachable. No orphan edges. MERMAID EDGE COMPLETENESS: for each function, scan steps/constraints for conditional paths (e.g., 'if empty → route to X', 'if NoData → skip to Y'). Each described conditional path MUST have a corresponding edge in the Mermaid graph with a descriptive label. Missing edges → add them. MERMAID EDGE LABEL VALIDATION: for each edge from a routing/NLU/decision node, verify the edge label covers ALL route types described in the corresponding function's step1. SEMANTIC ROUTING CONSISTENCY: for each new path added during evolution, check existing paths with similar operation semantics (create/read/update/delete). If a dedicated node exists for that operation type, new paths MUST route through it (enforces MF8 at generation time).
Generate.step4: EVOLUTION IMPACT SCAN (Evolve only): for each accepted evolution item (LEVEL_A/B/C), trace ALL references across the ENTIRE file: (a) FEATURE COUNT TRACE — scan all functions for hardcoded counts referencing changed lists (Error fields, menus, Explain steps). Update each to match actual count. (b) METADATA TRACE — if version bumped, update name field embedded vX.Y.Z, scan description for stale references. (c) DELEGATION TRACE — if modules added/removed, update NLU routing, delegation functions, Explain lists, menu options. (d) NLU INTENT TRACE — if NLU intents added/removed, execute STANDARD.description_hygiene.nlu_intent_checklist: confusion pairs, tiebreaker, enrichment, menu counts, Mermaid edges, AIGP.md intent_examples, node counts, Error text counts. (e) STANDARD RANGE TRACE: if evolution adds new STANDARD check items (e.g., adding I13 or PL25), scan all AIGP.md body sections and all module description fields for item range references that need updating. For each stale range found, auto-update the upper bound. Example: if I13 was added and description says 'I1-I12', update to 'I1-I13'. Output: impact_trace = { changed_elements[], affected_locations[], all_updated: boolean }. Log in generation_report. COUNT SYNC (all modes): verify all Error/fallback text counts match actual counts. Scan ALL Error fields.
Generate.step5: MULTI-FILE AUDIT (Pattern B+ only): verify each sub-module's id matches parent's delegation target. Verify shared parameters have consistent types across files. Run full MF1-MF16 checks: MF1 (version consistency), MF2 (delegation target match), MF3 (shared parameter type+default), MF4 (cross-file data contracts — FORMAT/NAME/VALUE/PATH: verify file path templates match across writer/reader modules), MF5 (data_file fallback), MF6 (AIGP.md sync + node count verification), MF7 (routing parameter symmetry), MF8 (semantic operation consistency), MF9 (cross-module node invocation — bidirectional: FORWARD check caller→sub-module route exists, REVERSE check sub-module route→has caller), MF10 (system_prompt consistency — all sub-modules include 'Respond in User\'s Language.' and 'Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.'), MF11 (Error strategy consistency — all Error fields follow retry→circuit-breaker→fallback→inform pattern), MF12 (tools union verification — main.tools[] = union of all sub-module tools[]), MF13 (persisted data schema versioning — all file_system write templates include schema_version, all reads handle missing via migration), MF14 (cross-module field consumer validation — trace producer→consumer field references, verify field names/types/semantics match, output dependency graph), MF15 (output contract enforcement — validate module outputs against declared json_schema at pipeline stage boundaries; N/A for contracts without json_schema), MF16 (tool directory consistency — verify tool_dirs references, mcp_server.json exposed_tools match, agent_card hash consistency; Pattern G only, N/A for others). Output: mf_audit = { mf1..mf16: PASS/FAIL }. Any MF FAIL → log as generation_warning. INCREMENTAL SYNC: if modules were skipped, verify skipped modules' contracts still match regenerated modules' updated contracts. Mismatch → force-regenerate.
Generate.step6: FRACTAL CHECK per STANDARD.C5 + AIGP_Protocol.md: Count functional nodes per file (total Mermaid nodes - Start - endNode). PATTERN CHECK: 2+ independent functions -> suggest Pattern B upgrade. PROGRESSIVE CHECK: 13-15 nodes -> ADVISORY with split suggestions. 16+ -> RECOMMENDED with concrete split plan. Respect fractal_exempt annotation. SHARED CHECK: 2+ modules reuse complex operations -> suggest Pattern C. PATTERN E/F: if plan specifies Pattern E (memory) -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='memory'. If plan specifies Pattern F (ecosystem) -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='orchestrate'. Protocol needs -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='protocol'. SKILL.md export -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='skill_export'. SKILL.md import -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='skill_import'. COMPOUND PATTERN COMPOSITION (B+E): when plan specifies both Pattern B (multi-file package) and Pattern E (memory/persistent state), compose as follows: (a) main.aisop.json = NLU router (Pattern B role), (b) functional sub-modules = independent function modules (Pattern B), (c) memory/ directory = persistent state files (Pattern E). DECISION: if 2+ sub-modules need memory access -> create dedicated memory.aisop.json sub-module (Pattern C shared); if only 1 sub-module needs memory -> integrate directly. Delegate to advisor with advisor_type='memory' for memory tier design. Ensure MF4 data contracts cover memory file paths across all memory-accessing modules. Report: 'Pattern {P}, {n} functional nodes, {k} modules.'
Generate.step7: UI GENERATION (conditional): if target AIGP.md declares a 'ui' block, generate ui_output per STANDARD.data_contracts.ui_output contract. For each component in ui.components: (a) validate data_source references: (i) a known STANDARD data_contract name (e.g., pipeline_metadata, research_context, simulation_report, quality_baseline), OR (ii) a pipeline_metadata sub-field (e.g., pipeline_metadata.token_tracking), OR (iii) a program-internal data source — check target program's module outputs for matching field names or data flow paths (e.g., a module's function output that feeds the ui component via Mermaid data flow). For program-internal sources, verify the producing module exists in AIGP.md modules[] and the referenced data path is traceable through the program's function steps, (b) for type='form', validate fields[] have name+type+default, (b2) for type='form', verify each field name maps to a writable program data path (parameter with file_system persistence or profile field) rather than a runtime-only parameter; flag runtime-only fields as UI_DATA_MISMATCH warning, (c) set rendering_engine from ui.rendering (default 'mcp_apps_v1'), (d) set interactivity=true if any component type='form'. Skip entirely if no ui block (backward compatible). Output ui_output object validated against json_schema.
Generate.step8: TOOL DIRECTORY GENERATION (Pattern G only). If AIGP.md contains tool_dirs field: (a) ANALYZE program tools[] and capabilities to determine required tool implementations, (b) SELECT RUNTIME — recommend language per need: Python (data/AI/ML), TypeScript (Web API/type safety), Go (concurrency/CLI), Rust (performance/WASM), Shell (system scripts/lightweight), (c) GENERATE SKELETON per selected language: README.md (purpose, interface, security constraints), dependency file (requirements.txt / package.json / go.mod / Cargo.toml / none), tool implementation stubs (entry script + function stubs with docstrings), interface definition (CLI args or JSON stdin/stdout), (d) GENERATE MCP ADAPTER — create mcp-tools/mcp_adapter.{ext} (stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 bridge: read stdin line-by-line, parse JSON, route to tool function by params.name, return JSON response, handle errors with code -32601/-32603), (e) GENERATE mcp_server.json — { schema_version: 'mcp-1.0', server_id, transport: 'stdio', runtime, entry_point, exposed_tools: [from tools[]], file_hashes: {SHA-256 per file}, governance: { aigp_protocol, trust_level, governance_hash: '(computed by ReviewStep)' } }, (f) GENERATE agent_card.json (A2A v1.0 optional) — { name, description, capabilities, endpoint }, (g) I13 PRE-CHECK — validate dependency pinning (no ^ or ~ prefixes), network declarations, file scope within tool_dirs/. If non-Pattern-G program: skip with note 'non-Pattern-G, no tool_dirs generation needed.'
Generate.constraints: Apply quality_checklist from STANDARD template. Evolution items dropped -> flag generation_warning. Fractal check emits progressive warnings, never FAIL on node count alone. MF4 NEW CONTRACTS: when evolution adds cross-module references (e.g., new function referencing another module's output), verify the referenced module's output format supports the new reference — create or update data contract documentation.
Generate.Error: Generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: minimal scaffold with TODO markers -> inform.

ThreeDimTest.step1: TIER DETECTION: Classify target — Simple (Pattern A, no file_system, <=6 functional nodes), Standard (Pattern A with >6 functional nodes OR Pattern B OR uses file_system), Professional (Pattern C/D, multi-file). NOTE: Pattern A with tools=[] and >6 nodes is classified as Standard (complex zero-tool systems have sufficient node count to warrant full testing).
ThreeDimTest.step2: COGNITIVE (C1-C7): C1 archetype fit, C2 node load (leaf<=8, convergence<=12), C3 abstraction consistency, C4 information flow, C5 fractal decomposition per AIGP_Protocol.md (functional node count = total - Start - endNode; progressive: 3-12 normal, 13-15 ADVISORY, 16+ RECOMMENDED; Pattern B+ main = router quality constraint; independence test; split priority: tool boundary > data flow phase > functional independence; respect fractal_exempt), C6 brain principle (LLM-native vs tool-required), C7 token budget compliance (per STANDARD C7 — verify AIGP.md runtime.token_budget is set and modules declare expected complexity).
ThreeDimTest.step3: INTRINSIC (I1-I13): I1 tool honesty (no phantom/undeclared), I2 security (5 guards; 4 guards if no file_system; prompt-injection focus if tools=[] per STANDARD I2 ZERO-TOOL ADAPTATION; per STANDARD tier_rules), I3 data integrity (atomic writes; N/A if tools=[] per STANDARD I3 EXEMPTION), I4 circuit-breaker (named fallbacks), I5 tool suitability (check Symbolic/Delegation/Zero-tool/Auto-Recognition exemptions per STANDARD I5), I6 resilience + semantic accuracy (structural: every function has Error field with retry→circuit-breaker→fallback→inform pattern; semantic: extract numbers from each Error/fallback text, cross-reference against actual counts in same function's steps — e.g., 'list all N features' where N must equal actual feature list length; flag mismatches as I6 FAIL), I7 parameter completeness + metadata consistency (parameters: each has type + agentic_prompt and is referenced in functions; metadata: if name contains vX.Y.Z must match version field; id module-name must match filename stem), I8 threat model awareness (for T3/T4 trust_level: verify Start.constraints address applicable threats from AT1-AT6 per STANDARD.security threat_taxonomy based on tools[] and trust_level; minimum: all programs address AT6, T3+ address AT4, network-tool programs address AT3; cross-reference per STANDARD.security I8), I9 supply chain integrity (for published/external programs: verify governance_hash; for trust_level >= T3: governance_hash REQUIRED per STANDARD I9), I10 trust level compliance (per STANDARD I10 — verify declared trust_level matches actual permissions and side_effects; flag over-privileged or under-declared), I11 program identity verification (per STANDARD.security I11 — for published programs with status='active' or trust_level >= T3: verify AIGP.md identity block contains valid program_id format and non-empty publisher; if signature present, verify against governance_hash), I12 tool annotation consistency (per STANDARD I12 — for each tool with annotations: verify read_only matches module side_effects, destructive requires T3+, open_world requires AT3 mitigation, idempotent matches module declaration; N/A for programs without tool annotations), I13 embedded code safety (per STANDARD I13 — verify tool_dirs/ code passes integrity, network, file scope, dependency, MCP proxy, and shell audit checks (6 sub-items per STANDARD I13); N/A for Pattern A-F). PROVISIONAL STATE: if pipeline_active=true (mid-pipeline execution where AIGP.md identity fields may not yet be finalized — e.g., verified_on not updated until ReviewStep), downgrade I11 missing-identity findings from WARNING to INFO with note 'identity fields provisional during pipeline execution — will be finalized in ReviewStep'. I2 INHERITANCE: for Pattern B+ sub-modules, if a guard is annotated as '(inherited from main router)' or '(inherited from main)', verify the main router file's Start.constraints contains the corresponding full guard implementation. If main has it → count as PASS for the sub-module. If main lacks it → count as FAIL (broken inheritance chain). Non-inheritable guards (A: Type guard, D: Path guard) must always be present locally in each sub-module — these require module-specific knowledge and cannot be inherited.
ThreeDimTest.step4: DETAIL (D1-D10): D1 step clarity (concrete verbs), D2 format consistency (step keys must follow sequential integer naming step1/step2/.../stepN; non-standard suffixes like step1_5/step2b/step4c are permitted for insertion ordering but count as minor format inconsistency scoring 4 not 5), D3 null propagation, D4 consistency + internal coherence (FORMAT: CamelCase Mermaid nodes, snake_case params; SEMANTIC: steps must not contradict own constraints; REFERENCE: any number mentioned in text must match actual count — menu items, feature lists, module counts; CROSS-FUNCTION: if function A names function B in text, B must exist in same file; CONSTRAINT CROSS-VALIDATION: for each module, compare constraints across ALL functions for logical consistency — (a) if any function declares 'Read-only' in constraints, no other function in same module may perform write operations; (b) if routing function declares 'Exactly one route', same function must not describe multiple simultaneous routes; (c) Mermaid edge count from decision node must match route count in function logic). D4-RANGE CROSS-REFERENCE CHECK (automated): scan all description fields across loaded AISOP files AND AIGP.md body text for item range patterns matching regex /[A-Z]+\d+-[A-Z]*\d+/ (e.g., 'C1-C7', 'I1-I13', 'PL1-PL25', 'MF1-MF16', 'QRG1-QRG5', 'AT1-AT6'). For each detected range, extract the prefix and upper bound. Cross-reference against loaded STANDARD quality_checklist item counts: C items (C1-C7), I items (I1-I13), D items (D1-D10), MF items (MF1-MF16), PL items (PL1-PL25), QRG items (QRG1-QRG5). If detected upper bound differs from STANDARD actual count (e.g., 'I1-I12' when STANDARD has I1-I13), flag as D4-RANGE-STALE with YELLOW severity: 'Range reference [found_range] is stale — STANDARD has [correct_range]'. D5 boundary rules, D6 multilingual, D7 metabolism (no dead code). D8 CHECK: verify AIGP.md exists in target directory. Check 12 required frontmatter fields. Check 3 required body sections. Check closing seal. For Pattern A: modules list must contain exactly one entry per STANDARD D8 pattern_a_note. Score: PASS if all present, FAIL if missing. D8-LICENSE SEMANTIC CHECK: if AIGP.md contains a license field, validate: (a) value is a recognized SPDX identifier (MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0-only, GPL-2.0-only, BSD-2-Clause, BSD-3-Clause, ISC, MPL-2.0, LGPL-3.0-only, AGPL-3.0-only, Unlicense, CC0-1.0, CC-BY-4.0, CC-BY-SA-4.0) or 'proprietary'. (b) value does NOT match protocol version pattern (reject 'AIGP V*' as license value — this is a common semantic error of confusing protocol version with license). (c) if license='proprietary', verify terms_url or contact field exists in AIGP.md. Score: valid SPDX → pass, invalid/missing for Store programs → YELLOW, protocol version in license field → YELLOW with specific message 'license field contains protocol version, not SPDX identifier'. D9 lifecycle status validity: verify AIGP.md status is one of draft/active/deprecated/archived. If deprecated: verify deprecated_date and successor fields exist. If archived: verify no Evolve/Modify operations attempted. D10 applicability condition completeness (per STANDARD D10 — verify AIGP.md applicability_condition exists with non-empty triggers; pipeline_exemption: deferred during Create pipeline).
ThreeDimTest.step5: PER-ITEM SCORING (1-5): Score each check item individually — 5=Full compliance (exemplary), 4=Minor issue (cosmetic/documentation), 3=Moderate issue (functional but improvable), 2=Significant issue (partially non-compliant), 1=Critical issue (missing/fundamentally broken). Dimension score = average of applicable item scores. GRADE THRESHOLDS: S(weighted>=4.50), A(>=3.50), B(>=2.50), C(>=1.50), D(<1.50). Per-dimension grades: A(avg>=4.50), B(>=3.50), C(>=2.50), D(<2.50). DYNAMIC WEIGHTING per STANDARD.dynamic_weights: if tier=='Simple', use C:0.35 I:0.35 D:0.30; if tier=='Standard' or 'Professional', use C:0.25 I:0.45 D:0.30. N/A HANDLING: when I8, I9, I10, I11, I12, and/or I13 are N/A (non-published programs, no external dependencies, no trust_level declared, no tool_dirs), exclude from Intrinsic dimension denominator — calculate I score as sum(applicable_scores) / count(applicable_items). Weighted score = C*Cw + I*Iw + D*Dw where Cw/Iw/Dw are tier-specific weights. AUTOMATED/SUBJECTIVE SPLIT: classify each check item per STANDARD automated check classification. Run automated checks first (D2, D4, D4-RANGE, D6, I7, MF1, MF6, MF15, PL14, I13a, I13b) using deterministic verification methods (regex, hash, schema validation). Produce lint_report with automated_checks results. Then run subjective assessment for remaining items (C1-C7, I1-I6, I8-I12, D1, D3, D5, D7-D10). Merge results: test_report.automated_results = lint_report, test_report.subjective_results = { items: [subjective_item_ids], average_score: weighted_average }, test_report.confidence_split = '{automated_count}/{total_count} items automated'.
ThreeDimTest.step6: MULTI-FILE AGGREGATE (Pattern B+): Grade each file independently. Composite score = average of per-file weighted scores (using tier-appropriate weights per STANDARD.dynamic_weights). Additionally check MF1-MF16 per STANDARD.multi_file_guidelines: MF1 (version consistency), MF2 (delegation target match), MF3 (shared parameter consistency), MF4 (cross-file data contracts — FORMAT/NAME/VALUE/PATH), MF5 (data_file fallback), MF6 (AIGP.md sync + node count verification), MF7 (routing parameter symmetry), MF8 (semantic operation consistency), MF9 (cross-module node invocation — bidirectional), MF10 (system_prompt consistency), MF11 (Error strategy consistency), MF12 (tools union verification), MF13 (persisted data schema versioning), MF14 (cross-module field consumer validation), MF15 (output contract enforcement — validate module outputs against declared json_schema at pipeline stage boundaries; N/A for contracts without json_schema), MF16 (tool directory consistency — verify tool_dirs references, mcp_server.json exposed_tools match, agent_card hash consistency; Pattern G only, N/A for others). Each MF violation counts as 1 issue in Intrinsic dimension of the main file's grade.
ThreeDimTest.constraints: TIER-ADJUSTED SEVERITY: Simple -> I2 is WARNING (at least type guard required), I3 skip if no file_system. Standard/Professional -> I2 is FAIL (all 5 guards), I3 is FAIL with file_system. MULTI-FILE: MF violations are Intrinsic-severity. Report both per-file grades and composite score.
ThreeDimTest.Error: Unparseable -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: partial grading -> inform.

TrafficClassify.step1: GREEN — auto-apply silently: Error field addition, input validation addition, boundary rule text, minor formatting.
TrafficClassify.step2: YELLOW — auto-apply + report: restructuring suggestions, metabolic cleaning, missing constraint additions.
TrafficClassify.step3: RED — pause for human: fractal restructuring, parameter space changes, flow redesign, grade drop >=2 levels. CROSS-FILE GRANULARITY: version/id-only changes across files -> GREEN. Same-module internal changes (e.g. generate + its helper) -> YELLOW. Cross-module data contract changes (e.g. research output format affects generate input) -> RED.
TrafficClassify.step4: Generate traffic_report: { green:[...], yellow:[...], red:[...], dim_grades:{C,I,D} }.
TrafficClassify.constraints: Cross-file classification per CROSS-FILE GRANULARITY rules in step3. Score impact >=1.0 -> RED.
TrafficClassify.Error: Classification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: default all items YELLOW -> inform.

PostGenCheck.step1: Check traffic_report for unresolved RED items. If present -> PauseHuman.
PostGenCheck.step2: Display summary: 'Green N | Yellow N | Red N | C:{g} I:{g} D:{g} | weighted:{score}'.
PostGenCheck.constraints: RED items only gate. Weighted score threshold is checked by main.aisop.json QualityGate after generation completes.
PostGenCheck.Error: Gate check fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: treat as RED present, route to PauseHuman -> inform.

PauseHuman.step2: Wait: approve, reject (continue with warning), or modify.
PauseHuman.Error: Timeout -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: treat as rejected, SaveDraft with warnings -> inform.

ApplyFixes.step1: Apply GREEN silently. Apply YELLOW and log each.
ApplyFixes.step2: FIX ORDER: isolated -> local per-function -> cross-function one-at-a-time. MULTI-FILE ORDER: sub-modules first, main last.
ApplyFixes.step3: VERSION RULES per STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management: modify_only→patch+1, evolve_level_c_only→patch+1, evolve_level_b→minor+1, evolve_level_a→major+1, mixed→highest applicable level wins.
ApplyFixes.step4: DESCRIPTION HYGIENE: if description contains more than 2 version history entries (e.g. 'v1.1.0 adds... v1.2.0 adds... v1.3.0 adds...'), compress into summary: keep current version changes in full, collapse older entries into single line 'Prior versions added: [comma-separated feature list].' SUMMARY CONSISTENCY: after description update, verify summary field still reflects current capabilities. If evolution added key features (new metrics, new modules, new tools) that summary doesn't mention, update summary. If evolution removed features that summary still mentions, update summary. Summary should remain 1-2 sentences — do not add version history. DERIVED REFERENCE AUDIT: full-file scan for internal consistency — (a) NAME VERSION SYNC: if name field contains embedded vX.Y.Z, update to match version field. Apply to ALL files in multi-file packages. (b) ERROR TEXT CONSISTENCY: scan all Error/fallback fields for hardcoded counts. Cross-reference each against actual list lengths in same function's steps. Fix stale counts. (c) MENU COUNT SYNC: Start/General menu item counts → match actual NLU intent/feature count. (d) EXPLAIN FEATURE SYNC: Explain.step1 feature list count → match Explain.Error referenced count. (e) CROSS-FUNCTION REFERENCE CHECK: if function A references function B by name in steps/constraints, verify B exists in the same file's functions. Auto-fix any mismatches found. Log all fixes in generation_report: { derived_fixes: [{location, old_value, new_value}] }.
ApplyFixes.step5: CUSTOM FIELD SEMANTIC CHECK: scan for fields like verified_on, author, tags. Preserve all. For verified_on specifically: if content changed beyond cosmetic fixes, append '(not re-verified after vX.Y.Z evolution)' to description as advisory. Do NOT modify the verified_on array itself.
ApplyFixes.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: in-memory backup. ATOMIC: .tmp rename. Max 3 fix cycles.
ApplyFixes.Error: Apply fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display patched JSON inline -> inform.

SaveDraft.step1: DIRECTORY RULES: Modify -> save in-place at {workspace_dir}/{name}_aigp/. Evolve -> save in-place (overwrite with .bak backup). Create -> create new directory {workspace_dir}/{name}_aigp/.
SaveDraft.step2: Save {file}.aisop.json. Atomic write: .tmp then rename. Backup existing as .v{old_version}.bak (e.g. main.aisop.json.v1.0.0.bak). AIGP.md CONTENT PREPARATION: if AIGP.md content was generated (Create mode) or updated (Evolve mode), prepare AIGP.md content but do NOT write — pass prepared content to ReviewStep for single-ownership write (per main.aisop.json ReviewStep.step3). MULTI-FILE: save each changed .aisop.json file with individual .bak. Unchanged files are NOT rewritten. INCREMENTAL: output content_hash for each written file in draft metadata for downstream stage-skip decisions. WRITE LOGGING: log write results in pipeline context: { files_written: [string], backups_created: [string], atomic_success: boolean, bytes_written: number }. If in pipeline context, append write_log to pipeline_metadata for ObservabilityStep telemetry.
SaveDraft.step3: Output: { draft_path, test_report, traffic_report, pattern, version, weighted_score }.
SaveDraft.constraints: Unresolved RED -> prepend WARNING in draft metadata.
SaveDraft.Error: Write fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display JSON in code block -> inform.

ReflectionCheck.step1: Verify: all Mermaid nodes have functions? Any RED residuals? Evolution items implemented?
ReflectionCheck.step2: Generate reflection_notes for ReviewFlow. Residuals -> note for next cycle.
ReflectionCheck.Error: Verification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip advisory, set notes = empty -> inform.
